core.title: derivative instrument
core.description: financial instrument that confers on its holders certain rights or obligations, whose value is derived from one or more underlying assets

core.title: credit derivative
core.description: derivative instrument that is a privately held, negotiable bilateral contract traded over-the-counter (OTC) between two parties in a creditor/debtor relationship, enabling the creditor to effectively transfer some or all of the risk of a debtor defaulting to a third party

core.title: rate-based derivative
core.description: derivative instrument where the holder has the right but may not have the obligation, depending on the nature of the instrument, to enter into the underlying contract, or pay or receive payment related to the underlying financial rate (or rate contract) on a specified future date based on a specified future rate and term

core.title: currency derivative
core.description: agreement to deliver and settle a given amount of money in one currency, in exchange for a given amount in another currency at an agreed rate of exchange

core.title: commodity derivative
core.description: derivative instrument whose primary underlying item is a physical commodity, or the price, or related index, or any other aspect related to a physical commodity

core.title: derivative terms
core.description: contractual terms specific to derivative contracts, including terms related to payments and delivery between parties

core.title: underlier
core.description: something that can be assigned a value in the marketplace that forms the basis for a derivative or pool-backed instrument

core.title: cashflow formula
core.description: formula for determining cashflows for a derivative instrument

core.title: option
core.description: derivative instrument that grants to the holder either the privilege to purchase or the privilege to sell the assets specified at a predetermined price or formula at or within a time period in the future

core.title: call option
core.description: option giving the buyer (holder) the right, but not the obligation, to buy the assets specified at a fixed price or formula, on or before a specified date

core.title: put option
core.description: option giving the buyer (holder) the right, but not the obligation, to sell the assets specified at a fixed price or formula, on or before a specified date

core.title: strike price
core.description: price at which a lot of the underlying asset may be bought or sold when the contract is exercised

core.title: moneyness
core.description: classifier for a derivative relating its strike price to the price of its underlying asset

core.title: covered call
core.description: call option in which the seller (investor) owns an equivalent amount of the underlying security

core.title: bond option
core.description: option giving the buyer (holder) the right, but not the obligation, to buy or sell (depending on whether it is a call or a put) a bond at a certain price on or before a specified date

core.title: currency option
core.description: option giving the buyer (holder) the right, but not the obligation, to buy or sell currency at a specified exchange rate during a specified period of time

core.title: equity option
core.description: option giving the buyer (holder) the right, but not the obligation, to buy (via a call option) or sell (via a put option) the underlying equity assets specified at a pre-determined price (i.e., the strike price, fixed or calculated), on or before a specified date (the expiration date)

core.title: swaption
core.description: over-the-counter option that confers the right but not the obligation, to enter into a swap transaction at some time in the future

core.title: Asian option
core.description: option whose exercise terms involve a payoff determined by the average underlying price (either the strike price or the settlement price) of the underlying asset over a predetermined period

core.title: barrier option
core.description: option whose final exercise depends upon the path taken by the price of an underlying instrument, i.e., whose payoff depends on whether or not the underlying asset has reached or exceeded a predetermined price

core.title: knock-in option
core.description: barrier option that is not triggered until a certain price threshold is met

core.title: knock-out option
core.description: barrier option with a built-in mechanism to expire as worthless if a specified price level in the underlying asset is reached

core.title: digital option
core.description: exotic option that has a pre-determined payout if the option is in-the-money and the payoff condition is satisfied

core.title: lookback option
core.description: exotic option that minimizes the uncertainties related to the timing of market entry, providing the holder the advantage of knowing history when determining when to exercise their option

core.title: compound option
core.description: exotic option for which the underlying asset is another option

core.title: future
core.description: derivative instrument that obligates the buyer to receive and the seller to deliver the assets specified at an agreed price, at some later point in time

core.title: forward
core.description: derivative instrument that is privately negotiated between parties to buy the underlier at a specified future date at the price specified in the contract

core.title: bond future
core.description: futures contract whose underlying asset is at least one bond

core.title: index future
core.description: futures contract whose underlying asset is at least one reference index or economic indicator

core.title: equity future
core.description: futures contract whose underlying asset is at least one equity security, specifically a publicly issued and traded share

core.title: currency future
core.description: futures contract whose underlying asset is an agreement to exchange a specified amount of one currency for another at some point in the future based on pricing stated in the contract

core.title: equity forward
core.description: forward contract to buy or sell the underlying equity stock, equity index, basket of equity stock, equity futures contract, or equity option at a specified future date at the price specified at the outset of the contract

core.title: forward rate agreement
core.description: agreement to exchange an interest rate commitment on a notional amount at a future date

core.title: currency forward
core.description: agreement to deliver and settle a given amount of money in one currency, in exchange for a given amount in another currency, at an agreed date in the future and at an agreed rate of exchange

core.title: swap
core.description: derivative instrument whereby counterparties agree to exchange periodic streams of cash flows or liabilities from two different financial instruments with each other

core.title: swap leg
core.description: terms defining and the commitment to fulfill cashflow requirements (e.g., interest payments, coupon payments, etc.) for a component of a swap

core.title: fixed leg
core.description: swap leg whose paying party is obligated to make payments from time to time over the term of a swap calculated by reference to a fixed annual rate or consisting of at least one payment of a fixed amount

core.title: floating leg
core.description: swap leg whose paying party is obligated to make payments from time to time over the term of a swap calculated by reference to some variable rate, calculated based on the change in value of some underlying asset, or consisting of at least one payment of some floating amount

core.title: interest rate swap
core.description: swap in which the reference (underlier) for at least one leg is an interest rate

core.title: currency swap
core.description: foreign exchange agreement between two parties to exchange a given amount of one currency for another currency for spot delivery or for forward delivery at an agreed rate after a specified period of time

core.title: basis swap
core.description: swap in which payment streams are referenced to different bases

core.title: overnight index swap
core.description: swap in which the periodic payments for one leg are based on an overnight interest rate index multiplied by the same notional amount upon which payments for the other leg of the swap are based

core.title: total return swap
core.description: return swap where the seller agrees to pay the other party the difference in value of some underlying asset multiplied by an agreed-upon notional value should the asset value increase between specified periods of time

core.title: equity swap
core.description: swap whose payments are linked to the change in value of underlying equities (e.g. shares, basket of equities or index) or their cashflow(s)

core.title: commodity swap
core.description: commodity derivative that includes, without limitation, any swap for which the primary underlying notional item is a physical commodity, or the price, or behavior of the price, or the level of a commodity index, or other aspect related to a physical commodity

core.title: credit default swap
core.description: bilateral contract in which one party (protection seller) agrees to provide payment to the other party (protection buyer) should a credit event occur against the underlying, which could be a specified debt (the reference obligation), a specific debt issuer (reference entity), a basket of reference entities and/or reference obligations, or a credit index (reference index)

core.title: triggering event
core.description: event that relates to or triggers some aspect of a credit default swap

core.title: credit event notice
core.description: irrevocable written or verbal notice that states that a triggering event has occurred

core.title: deliverable asset
core.description: asset that must be delivered as a part of the process of settling a credit default swap

core.title: loan credit default swap
core.description: credit default swap whose underlying reference obligation is limited strictly to syndicated secured loans, rather than any type of corporate debt

core.title: credit default swap index
core.description: index whose underlying reference obligations are credit default swap instruments

core.title: credit protection terms
core.description: legal terms that define triggering events and associated conditions related to settlement

core.title: counterparty
core.description: party to a contract with whom one negotiates on a given agreement

core.title: master agreement
core.description: contract between named parties that outlines the terms and conditions designed to apply to a number of accounts, transactions, or other activities between the parties, and that consolidates and provides overarching terms for separate but related agreements

core.title: derivative master agreement
core.description: master agreement covering derivatives transactions to be carried out between the parties to this contract

core.title: swap dealer
core.description: non-depository institution such as one that deals in swaps, makes a market in swaps, regularly enters into swaps with counterparties as an ordinary course of business for its own account, and engages in any activity causing the person to be commonly known in the trade as a dealer/market maker in swaps

core.title: clearing house
core.description: financial service provider that is exchange affiliated and provides clearing services, including the validation, delivery, and settlement of financial transactions, for financial intermediaries

core.title: paying party
core.description: party responsible for making payments in a transaction specified in a contract

core.title: receiving counterparty
core.description: party that receives payments in a transaction specified in a contract

core.title: structured product
core.description: security whose cash flow characteristics depend upon one or more indices or that have embedded forwards or options or securities where an investor's investment return and the issuer's payment obligations are contingent on, or highly sensitive to, changes in the value of underlying assets, indices, interest rates or cash flows
